features: TL-UL CSR read/write access with basic protocol sanity
32-bit GPIO direction control via DIR register
DATA_OUT driving and gpio_oe_o behavior for output pins
DATA_IN synchronization latency and stable sampling behavior
Per-pin interrupt enable and state handling
Level-high interrupt behavior with re-assert after W1C clear
Level-low interrupt behavior with re-assert after W1C clear
Masked write atomic update behavior for lower and upper 16-bit halves
Reset behavior for CSRs and outputs
Per-pin interrupt output reflection on intr_o

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_and_csr_map_sanity
directed_tests.intent: Apply reset, verify TL-UL accessibility, confirm readable/writable CSRs at expected offsets, and check default-safe values for DATA_OUT, DIR, INTR_ENABLE, INTR_STATE, gpio_oe_o, intr_o, and alert_o.

directed_tests.name: dir_output_drive_basic
directed_tests.intent: Program a subset of pins as outputs, write DATA_OUT patterns, and verify gpio_o and gpio_oe_o reflect DIR and DATA_OUT for multiple bit positions including boundary pins 0, 15, 16, and 31.

directed_tests.name: dir_input_tristate_basic
directed_tests.intent: Program a subset of pins as inputs, drive gpio_i externally, and verify gpio_oe_o deasserts while DATA_IN eventually reflects synchronized input values after the expected latency.

directed_tests.name: masked_write_lower_half
directed_tests.intent: Exercise MASKED_OUT_LOWER with partial masks to confirm atomic update semantics on DATA_OUT[15:0] without disturbing unmasked bits.

directed_tests.name: masked_write_upper_half
directed_tests.intent: Exercise MASKED_OUT_UPPER with partial masks to confirm atomic update semantics on DATA_OUT[31:16] without disturbing unmasked bits.

directed_tests.name: intr_test_sets_state
directed_tests.intent: Write one-hot and multi-bit values to INTR_TEST and verify corresponding INTR_STATE bits set and intr_o follows INTR_STATE & INTR_ENABLE.

directed_tests.name: rising_edge_interrupt
directed_tests.intent: Configure a pin for input, enable rising-edge interrupt mode and INTR_ENABLE, toggle gpio_i low-to-high, and verify INTR_STATE and intr_o assert after synchronizer latency.

directed_tests.name: falling_edge_interrupt
directed_tests.intent: Configure a pin for input, enable falling-edge interrupt mode and INTR_ENABLE, toggle gpio_i high-to-low, and verify INTR_STATE and intr_o assert after synchronizer latency.

directed_tests.name: level_high_interrupt_reassert
directed_tests.intent: Enable level-high interrupt on a pin, drive gpio_i high, clear INTR_STATE with W1C, and verify the interrupt re-asserts while the level remains high.

directed_tests.name: level_low_interrupt_reassert
directed_tests.intent: Enable level-low interrupt on a pin, drive gpio_i low, clear INTR_STATE with W1C, and verify the interrupt re-asserts while the level remains low.

directed_tests.name: multi_pin_interrupt_and_masking
directed_tests.intent: Stimulate multiple pins with mixed edge and level modes to confirm per-pin independence, simultaneous interrupt behavior, and correct masking through INTR_ENABLE.

directed_tests.name: w1c_clear_behavior
directed_tests.intent: Set INTR_STATE via edge or INTR_TEST, clear selected bits using W1C writes, and verify only written-one bits clear while others remain set.

random_tests.name: constrained_gpio_register_fuzz
random_tests.intent: Run short randomized TL-UL CSR sequences over DIR, DATA_OUT, INTR_ENABLE, INTR_STATE, INTR_TEST, and interrupt mode registers with a scoreboard model, using constrained legal accesses and a small number of seeds to fit the 60-minute budget.

random_tests.name: random_gpio_stimulus_with_interrupt_scoreboard
random_tests.intent: Randomly toggle gpio_i on a limited set of pins while randomly enabling edge/level modes and checking expected intr_o and INTR_STATE behavior with synchronizer-aware latency modeling.

random_tests.name: masked_write_randomized_patterns
random_tests.intent: Generate random mask/data combinations for MASKED_OUT_LOWER and MASKED_OUT_UPPER, compare against a reference model of DATA_OUT updates, and verify readback consistency.

risk_areas.area: Input synchronizer latency and edge detection timing
risk_areas.reason: The design uses a two-stage synchronizer and edge detection on synchronized values, so off-by-one-cycle expectations are a common source of false failures and real bugs.

risk_areas.area: Interrupt re-assertion for level-sensitive modes after W1C clear
risk_areas.reason: Level interrupts must reassert while the condition persists; this is a subtle state-machine behavior with high bug potential.

risk_areas.area: Masked write atomicity and bit-lane mapping
risk_areas.reason: Incorrect mask/data bit ordering or partial update behavior can silently corrupt outputs and is easy to miss without directed checks.
Boundary bits and mixed-mode operation can expose indexing errors, especially around pins 15/16 and 31.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL CSR decode and register offset correctness
risk_areas.reason: A small CSR block can still have address decode or access-type issues; basic bus smoke plus a few negative checks are sufficient within budget.
Only one alert output is present and the excerpt does not define functional triggers, so it is lower risk and should be observed for idle/stability rather than exhaustively tested.
